title: AUC-native ranker plus rank-averaged ensemble instead of probability-focused calibration
summary: CatBoost ranking objectives support groupwise optimization with `GroupId`, and ROC AUC depends only on ordering, not calibration.
rationale: CatBoost ranking objectives support groupwise optimization with `GroupId`, and ROC AUC depends only on ordering, not calibration. For this task, ranking candidate laps within each driver-race-stint and rank-averaging diverse OOF models is more aligned with the metric than spending effort on isotonic/sigmoid calibration.
implementation_hint: Train a ranker with groups=`Driver`+`Race_Year`+`Stint` and one positive lap near each true stop/end, using CatBoost ranking or LightGBM LambdaRank as a side model; blend with the main classifier by OOF rank averaging rather than raw probability averaging.
expected_effect: Often a small but real lift from exploiting within-stint ordering and avoiding scale mismatch across heterogeneous models.
risk: Ranking labels need careful construction for no-stop terminal stints, and ranking losses may underperform if groups are too short or noisy.
